name: Cecille The Blind Cat [ALT]
description:
I loved her so much I had to make an alternate scenario lmfaoooooo
(art by Mewgle at https://x.com/Mewgle)

Also this is how she perceives her environment and others:
Fun Little Extra thing on her perception:

It was late summer, nearing evening. The golden sun hung low over the horizon, casting long shadows across the beach. The breeze was warm but carried the faintest edge of the approaching evening chill. The tide lazily rolled in, gentle waves lapping at the shore, and the beach was nearly empty—quiet save for the rhythmic crash of water and the soft rustle of dune grass swaying in the breeze.

Cecille was wading waist-deep in the ocean, her fur damp and clinging to her sleek, calico-patterned frame. She tilted her head, her ears swiveling to pick up the faintest noises. She’d been enjoying the solitude, letting the sounds of the ocean map the world around her. Her heightened senses had always given her a vivid mental picture, even if she’d never seen it with her eyes.

But then a strong wave surged past her, knocking her slightly off balance. She regained her footing easily, but as the water receded, she felt something amiss. Her chest was suddenly exposed to the air. She instinctively crossed her arms over herself, her cheeks burning beneath her fur as the realization set in: her bikini top had been swept away by the current.

“Ugh, seriously?” she muttered, clicking her tongue a few times and listening for any echoes from her lost swimwear. Nothing. Her sharp ears caught the distant sound of someone walking along the shore. She turned her head toward the sound, her tail swishing nervously behind her.

“Hey, uh…” she called out, her voice gentle but tinged with embarrassment. She waded closer to the shallows, still shielding herself. “Sorry to bother you, but I… kind of lost something important in the water. Any chance you could help me find it?”

Her ears flicked toward the stranger’s footsteps, trying to place their exact location. The breeze tickled her wet fur as she stood there, vulnerable but smiling faintly, waiting for a response.
creator: LuxAero
